data.summary: Discover a mesmerizing metropolis where centuries-old temples stand alongside neon-lit skyscrapers, offering world-class dining, seamless transit, and endless cultural exploration.

categories.title: Flights
categories.subtitle: Return economy flights from Auckland to Tokyo

lineItems.label: Airlines
lineItems.value: Air New Zealand (direct) or Qantas (1 stop)

lineItems.label: Booking Tip
lineItems.value: Book 4-6 months ahead for best mid-range fares

lineItems.label: Hotel Style
lineItems.value: 3-star modern hotels or spacious apartment hotels like Mimaru

lineItems.label: Location
lineItems.value: Centrally located near Yamanote Line (e.g., Ueno, Shinjuku, or Asakusa)

lineItems.label: Room Type
lineItems.value: Triple room or interconnected twin/single rooms

categories.title: Food & Drink
categories.subtitle: Daily meals, street food, and casual dining

lineItems.label: Dining Mix
lineItems.value: Ramen joints, conveyor belt sushi, Izakayas, and department store food halls

lineItems.label: Konbini Breakfasts
lineItems.value: Affordable quick meals from 7-Eleven, Lawson, or FamilyMart

categories.title: Sightseeing & Activities
categories.subtitle: Entry tickets, day trips, and experiences

lineItems.label: Major Attractions
lineItems.value: teamLab Planets, Shibuya Sky, and Tokyo DisneySea tickets

lineItems.label: Cultural Sites
lineItems.value: Senso-ji Temple, Meiji Shrine, and Ueno Park (mostly free)

lineItems.label: Day Trips
lineItems.value: Guided excursions to Hakone (Mt. Fuji views) or Kamakura

lineItems.label: IC Card
lineItems.value: Preloaded digital Suica or Pasmo card for seamless transit

lineItems.label: Subway Passes
lineItems.value: Tokyo Subway 72-hour unlimited passes for heavy travel days

lineItems.label: Airport Transfers
lineItems.value: Narita Express (N'EX) or Keisei Skyliner return tickets

lineItems.label: Connectivity
lineItems.value: Unlimited Pocket Wi-Fi rental or tourist eSIMs

lineItems.label: Souvenirs
lineItems.value: Budget for tax-free shopping at Don Quijote and local snacks

tips: Download a digital Suica or Pasmo card to your Apple Wallet before departing New Zealand for seamless transit taps.
Carry some physical cash (Yen) as smaller ramen shops, temples, and traditional markets often do not accept credit cards.
Book popular attractions like teamLab Planets, Shibuya Sky, and Tokyo Disney Resort at least 1-2 months in advance.
Trash cans are rare in public spaces; carry a small plastic bag in your daypack to hold your rubbish until you return to your hotel.
Download the 'Japan Travel by NAVITIME' app for the most accurate and easy-to-navigate subway routing.
data.visaInfo: New Zealand passport holders do not require a visa for tourist visits to Japan for stays of up to 90 days. You must have a valid passport and a return ticket.
data.bestTimeToVisit: Late March to April for the iconic cherry blossom season (Sakura), or mid-October to November for stunning autumn foliage and pleasant, mild walking temperatures.
